## Generated Files

Several files in this repo are fully generated by `rule sync-targets`. Do not edit them by hand.

### Rule-generated files (do not edit directly)

| File | Generator config |
|---|---|
| `AGENTS.md` | `rule-targets.yaml` → `context-engine-agents` target |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | `rule-targets.yaml` → `context-engine-copilot-instructions` target |
| `.clinerules/10-core-rules.md` | `rule-targets.yaml` |
| `.agents/instructions/*.instructions.md` | `rule-targets.yaml` → `50-agents-instructions.yaml` |
| `.agents/prompts/*.prompt.md` | `rule-targets.yaml` → `30-agents-prompts.yaml` |
| `.agents/agents/*.agent.md` | `rule-targets.yaml` → `45-agents-agents.yaml` |
| `memory-viewers/README.md` | `memory-viewers/rule-targets.yaml` |
| `memory-api/README.md` | `memory-api/rule-targets.yaml` |
| `repo_map.toon` | `cargo run -p peek-cli -- . --repo-map --output repo_map.toon` (not rule-managed) |

### Rule-managed file workflow

To update the content of a rule-generated file:

1. Find or create the rule entry: `rule list --section <section>` or `rule create ...`.
2. Edit the rule entry body: `rule update <id> --body "..."` or edit `.rule/rules/<id>/body.md` directly.
3. Regenerate: `cargo run -p rule-cli --bin rule -- sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml`.
4. Stage the regenerated file and the updated rule store together: `git add AGENTS.md .rule/rules/<id>/`.
5. Commit — the pre-commit hook will verify the output is consistent.

### Repo map

`repo_map.toon` is generated by the Rust `peek` CLI, not by the rule system:

```bash
cargo run -p peek-cli -- . --repo-map --output repo_map.toon
git add repo_map.toon
```

Refresh it whenever workspace members (crates) or agent files change.
